story.description: A clip of rare natural phenomena, unusual light, ice, water, or seasonal events: serene full moon over mountain ridge
story.hook: Auroras start with charged particles from the sun.
story.lead: Auroras start with charged particles from the sun. Watch the moving color, because charged particles follow Earth's magnetic field toward the upper atmosphere. When they hit oxygen and nitrogen, the air releases light. The sky is reacting to space weather. Which rare sky event next?
story.script: Auroras start with charged particles from the sun. Watch the moving color, because charged particles follow Earth's magnetic field toward the upper atmosphere. When they hit oxygen and nitrogen, the air releases light. The sky is reacting to space weather. Which rare sky event next?

story.description: A clip of cats / kittens: adorable sleeping cat on soft sofa
story.hook: Cats show why the ears matters.
story.lead: Cats sleep up to 20 hours a day because they’re built to ambush prey. That’s why you see them doze mid-play—energy banking for the real hunt. Ever notice how their ears twitch even when they’re out cold? That’s their survival radar still on. So next time they nap in your sunbeam, respect the nap.
story.script: Cats sleep up to 20 hours a day because they’re built to ambush prey. That’s why you see them doze mid-play—energy banking for the real hunt. Ever notice how their ears twitch even when they’re out cold? That’s their survival radar still on. So next time they nap in your sunbeam, respect the nap.

story.description: A clip of cats / kittens: close up on cat sleeping on couch
story.hook: Cats use grooming to reset their scent map.
story.lead: Cats use grooming to reset their scent map. Watch the tongue and paw movements, because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat. That can help a cat settle after a tense moment and keep the fur ready for the next move. The cleanup is also a signal. Which pet behavior should we decode next?
story.script: Cats use grooming to reset their scent map. Watch the tongue and paw movements, because grooming spreads familiar scent across the coat. story.description: A clip of rivers, streams, currents, waterfalls, or freshwater systems: serene mountain river flowing in clear waters
story.hook: Rivers sort sediment while they bend.
story.lead: Rivers sort sediment while they bend. Watch the edge of the flow, because slower water drops heavier material while faster water keeps cutting. That sorting is how a quiet bend slowly redraws the map. Which river edge should we compare next?
story.script: Rivers sort sediment while they bend. Watch the edge of the flow, because slower water drops heavier material while faster water keeps cutting. That sorting is how a quiet bend slowly redraws the map. Which river edge should we compare next?

story.description: A clip of rare natural phenomena, unusual light, ice, water, or seasonal events: the moon is shown in the dark with the words the moon is full
story.hook: Auroras start with charged particles from the sun.
story.lead: Auroras start with charged particles from the sun. Watch the moving color, because charged particles follow Earth's magnetic field toward the upper atmosphere. When they hit oxygen and nitrogen, the air releases light. The sky is reacting to space weather. Which rare sky event next?
story.script: Auroras start with charged particles from the sun. Watch the moving color, because charged particles follow Earth's magnetic field toward the upper atmosphere. When they hit oxygen and nitrogen, the air releases light. The sky is reacting to space weather. Which rare sky event next?
